options: The boy who won the prize is my brother.

options: To avoid coming to the point directly
To fight someone in a garden

options: Although it was raining, he went out.
He went out despite the rain.
It was raining but he went out.
He went out in the rain.

options: She said she is coming the next day.
She said she was coming the following day.
She said she will come tomorrow.

questions.question: Which of the following sentences contains a dangling modifier?
options: Running fast, the bus was caught by Emeka.
Running fast, Emeka caught the bus.
Emeka, running fast, caught the bus.
Emeka ran fast and caught the bus.

options: The students are punished by the teacher.
The students were punished by the teacher.

options: She sang while he played the guitar.

options: To work or study late into the night

options: If I was you, I would apologise.
If I were you, I would apologise.
If I am you, I would apologise.
If I be you, I would apologise.

questions.question: The synonym of VERBOSE is:

options: The man which called is my father.
The man who called is my father.

The man whose called is my father.

Those who start early or act promptly gain an advantage

To be very sensitive to smells

options: The variety of language used in a particular social situation or profession

questions.question: Choose the sentence in which 'since' is used as a conjunction of time:
options: Since he is rich, he can afford it.
I haven't seen her since she left.

questions.question: The word EQUIVOCAL means:
options: Open to more than one interpretation; deliberately vague

options: He came early; but she left late.
He came early; she left late.
